title: QA Core Spec FAIL: validation.gates is [0,1,2,3] missing gates 4 and 5 (BAD_GATE_SEQUENCE)
_fixture_purpose: Demonstrates BAD_GATE_SEQUENCE: validation.gates=[0,1,2,3] is missing gates 4 (invariant_diff check) and 5 (canonical hash / Merkle integrity). These are the two gates that make QA certs tamper-evident and externally auditable. A spec declaring fewer than 6 gates cannot be a kernel spec — it produces certs that lack the invariant_diff gate (Gate 4, which catches silent invariant violations) and the hash integrity gate (Gate 5, which closes the replay chain). This fixture proves the validator enforces the full gate sequence.

fail_ledger.message: validation.gates=[0,1,2,3] does not equal the required sequence [0,1,2,3,4,5]. Missing: Gate 4 (invariant_diff check — catches silent invariant violations without explicit fail_type) and Gate 5 (canonical hash / Merkle integrity — closes the replay chain and makes certs tamper-evident). A QA kernel spec must declare all six gates. Truncated gate sequences produce certs that cannot be externally audited.
